mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values indicate strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases yield negative 20-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, breaking the earnings transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 320.0 reflects entrenched inflationary pressures that force aggressive monetary tightening expectations, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 320.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of observations, indicating market pricing of inflation is decoupled from price levels.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting sustained earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio < 0.5 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate economic activity, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production index values signal contracting manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recessionary phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 70% of cases, indicating mean-reversion dominates the supply signal.
